message:
chore: sync test vectors and issue tracker

- Regenerate CBOR golden test vectors
- Update beads issue tracker state

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-32gn","title":"[IDEA-17.2] Unit tests for schema hash determinism","description":"## Coverage
- Same schema → same hash across runs.
- Different schema version → different hash.
- Deterministic vector ordering.

## Logging
- Record schema id + hash in JSON logs.
## Logging
- Emit structured JSONL per `docs/STANDARD_Testing_Logging.md` with `test_name`, `module`, `phase`, `correlation_id`, and `result`.
- Validate with `fcp_conformance::schemas::validate_e2e_log_jsonl` (use `fcp-testkit` LogCapture in unit tests).

 {"id":"bd-32k7","title":"TEST-AUDIT: Coverage inventory + mock usage map","description":"# TEST-AUDIT: Coverage inventory + mock usage map

## Goal
Produce a concrete coverage and mock-usage inventory so we can target real-component tests first.

## Deliverables
- Coverage map by crate and module: fcp-core, fcp-protocol, fcp-crypto, fcp-store, fcp-mesh, fcp-sandbox, fcp-host, fcp-cli, fcp-sdk, connectors.
- Mock/fake usage map: enumerate tests that rely on mock_server, wiremock, fake clients, or test doubles.
- Gap list for untested critical paths (protocol parse, crypto verification, revocation freshness, audit/receipt logic, sandbox enforcement).

## Notes
- This task does NOT delete existing mock tests; it produces a replacement plan for no-mock suites.

## Acceptance Criteria
- One report file under docs/testing/ with per-crate coverage estimate and mock usage list.
- Each gap maps to a follow-up bead in this epic.
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"TealCliff","created_at":"2026-01-27T18:24:19.424581460Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T18:31:00.478824103Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T18:31:00.478700113Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-32k7","depends_on_id":"bd-3p99","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T18:24:19.434679965Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-32sh","title":"[IDEA-28.1] Implement usage metric schema + host aggregation","description":"## What
Implement usage metric schema and host aggregation pipeline.

## How
- Define `UsageMetric` and optional `CostEstimate` objects.
- SDK helper to emit metrics from connectors.
- Host aggregates per zone and stores rolling windows.

## Success Criteria
- Aggregated metrics accessible via API/CLI.

## Acceptance Criteria (Refined)
- Aggregation windows are deterministic and bounded.
- Metrics emit confidence labels for cost estimates.

-{"id":"bd-334n","title":"Connector Lifecycle Manager: pin/canary/rollback","description":"# Connector Lifecycle Manager: pin/canary/rollback

## Goal
Enable safe connector upgrades with version pinning, staged rollout, and automatic rollback on health regressions.

## Why
- Prevents fleet-wide outages from bad releases.
- Supports offline environments via pinned artifacts.
- Gives operators deterministic control over connector versions.

## Scope
- Release metadata + rollout policy schema.
- Host rollout controller with health gating and audit events.
- CLI workflow and status reporting.

## Non-goals
- Full registry redesign; lifecycle consumes existing registry metadata.

## Deliverables
- ReleaseManifest + RolloutPolicy schemas (signed).
- Host state machine: pinned -> canary -> full -> rollback.
- CLI commands for pin/unpin, rollout set/status, rollback.
- Unit/integration tests + E2E rollout script with structured logs.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Canary rollout can be configured per connector.
- Automatic rollback triggers on health thresholds and is auditable.
- Operators can pin/unpin versions deterministically.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:03:07.205546340Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T05:25:25.716800603Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fcp2","host","lifecycle"]}

## Goal
Enable safe connector upgrades with version pinning, staged rollout, and automatic rollback on health regressions.

## Why
- Prevents fleet-wide outages from bad releases.
- Supports offline environments via pinned artifacts.
- Gives operators deterministic control over connector versions.

## Scope

- CLI workflow and status reporting.

- CLI commands for pin/unpin, rollout set/status, rollback.

## Acceptance Criteria

 {"id":"bd-33a5","title":"Expand fcp-core policy.rs test coverage (1 to 57 tests)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"chore","created_at":"2026-01-30T04:37:30.293988112Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T04:37:41.222119200Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T04:37:41.222046384Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-33no","title":"Supply Chain Verification: Attestation + SBOM Gate","description":"# Supply Chain Verification: Attestation + SBOM Gate

## Goal
Block untrusted connector installs/updates by verifying signed supply-chain attestations and SBOMs before activation, with deterministic evidence bundles for audit and offline installs.

## Why (User Impact)
- Operators get cryptographic assurance that the binary matches audited provenance.
- Prevents downgrade/typosquat or mixed-identity artifacts from entering the mesh.
- Makes offline installs safer via cached, signed evidence.

## Scope
- Attestation + SBOM object schemas and signature verification.
- Host-side verification pipeline and policy gate during install/upgrade.
- CLI verify/report flows and machine-readable evidence bundles.
- Deterministic, redactable logs and receipts suitable for audits.

## Non-goals
- Implementing a full vulnerability scanner (we ingest/report external results).
- Replacing the registry; this is a gate on install/upgrade only.

## Deliverables
- Canonical schemas for SupplyChainAttestation and SBOM objects.
- Host verification pipeline (signature, provenance binding, policy checks).
- Evidence bundle object with stable hashes and redacted fields.
- CLI commands for verify/report/install gating.
- Unit/integration tests + E2E scripts with structured JSON logs.

## Security/Policy Notes
- Fail closed by default (missing/invalid attestation or SBOM blocks install).
- Support policy knobs: require_attestation, min_slsa_level, trusted_builders, allow_unsigned (dev-only).
- Verification must be deterministic (same inputs -> same evidence bundle hash).
- Never log secrets or raw tokens; redact sensitive fields in logs/evidence.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Valid attestation+SBOM installs; invalid/unsigned is rejected unless explicit policy allows.
- Evidence bundle includes all verification steps, hashes, trust roots, and timestamps.
- Errors are actionable, deterministic, and redact secrets.
- Unit/integration tests cover positive/negative paths; E2E script passes in CI.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"MistyFalcon","created_at":"2026-02-01T02:58:43.109587772Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T18:32:03.340094551Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fcp2","security","supply-chain"]}
 {"id":"bd-33qn","title":"[IDEA-08.3] Retry taxonomy tests + examples","description":"## Goal
Provide tests and examples for retry taxonomy helpers.

## Details
- Integration test with fake HTTP server returning retryable errors.
- Example showing how connectors should use retry policy in send/invoke paths.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tests verify backoff and Retry-After behavior.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T19:36:14.027454485Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T17:02:34.429813029Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T17:02:34.429726629Z","close_reason":"Added retry taxonomy integration test + example usage","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-33qn","depends_on_id":"bd-3drg","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T19:36:14.034844115Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
